internships.title: CDFA High School Internship Program
internships.company: California Department of Food and Agriculture
internships.description: Year-round internships in various CDFA divisions for students interested in agriculture, food safety, and environmental stewardship. Interns work 8–30 hours/week for 10–16 weeks gaining real government agency experience.
internships.whyApply: Gain agriculture and food policy experience at California's state agricultural agency.

internships.title: USC Exploring Entrepreneurship Pre-College Program
internships.description: Four-week pre-college course giving high school students insights into entrepreneurship through daily lectures, case studies, and hands-on business projects at USC's campus. Build a real startup pitch.
internships.whyApply: Learn entrepreneurship at a top university with real case studies and business pitch projects.

internships.title: Brighter Futures OC High School Career Exploration
internships.description: 6-week career exploration program running June–July in hybrid format. Students complete 90 hours total (15 hrs/week) exploring various career paths through workshops, site visits, and mentorship in Orange County.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive career exploration program in Orange County with hands-on industry exposure.

internships.title: Fresno BHC Youth Internship Program
internships.description: Paid internship for Fresno-area youth interested in community organizing, public health advocacy, and social justice. Students work on real community health initiatives while developing leadership and advocacy skills.
internships.whyApply: Paid community health internship building advocacy and leadership skills in Central California.

internships.title: OPALS High School Internship Program
internships.company: UC San Diego Institute of Engineering in Medicine
internships.description: Six-week STEM research internship where high schoolers work alongside UCSD researchers, graduate students, and industry collaborators on interdisciplinary projects at the intersection of engineering, medicine, and artificial intelligence. Concludes with a research presentation or poster session.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on interdisciplinary research at UCSD combining engineering, medicine, and AI — excellent for aspiring biomedical engineers.

internships.title: Research Experience for High School Students (REHS)
internships.company: San Diego Supercomputer Center (SDSC) at UC San Diego
internships.description: Eight-week volunteer research program introducing high schoolers to high-performance computing, data science, and computational research. Students work in pairs or small groups with UCSD mentors on established research projects, gaining exposure to career options in computational science.
internships.whyApply: Learn high-performance computing and data science at one of the nation's leading supercomputer centers.

internships.title: STRIDE Summer Research Training Program
internships.company: UC Riverside Bourns College of Engineering
internships.description: Five-week summer research training program for 60 Inland Empire high school students. Trainees work in UCR labs on projects in bioengineering, materials science, tissue engineering, neuroscience, stem cell biology, and toxicology. Includes workshops, career panels, and mentoring.
internships.whyApply: Outstanding research opportunity for Inland Empire students — work in real UCR labs on cutting-edge stem cell and bioengineering research.

internships.title: Youth Employment Summer Success (Y.E.S.S.) Program
internships.description: Five-week paid summer employment program placing 100 Stockton youth (ages 16-19) in City departments. Includes work readiness training, professional development, and hands-on experience in municipal government operations across various departments.
internships.whyApply: Well-paid city government internship at $23/hr — excellent introduction to public sector careers in the Central Valley.

internships.title: Summer Jobs Program
internships.company: Boys & Girls Clubs of Kern County
internships.description: Paid summer internship with local employers for Bakersfield-area teens (ages 15-18). Preceded by a Career Launch workforce readiness training covering financial literacy, business etiquette, First Aid/CPR, and food handler certification. Interns gain real workplace experience with professional development on Fridays.
internships.whyApply: Comprehensive workforce readiness program with paid internship — great for Central Valley teens building career skills.

internships.title: Bakersfield Youth Jobs Program (BYJP)
internships.company: Kern Community Foundation / City of Bakersfield
internships.description: Youth employment program placing Bakersfield-area young people (ages 16-30) into meaningful employment opportunities across local businesses and organizations. Participants gain real-world work experience while developing professional skills.
internships.whyApply: City-backed paid employment program connecting Central Valley youth with local employers across multiple industries.

internships.title: Summer Legal Fellowship Program
internships.company: Center for Youth Development through Law / UC Berkeley School of Law
internships.description: Full-time two-month paid summer program for underserved high schoolers from Oakland, Berkeley, and Richmond. Includes law classes on the UC Berkeley Law campus, law-related internship placements, social justice curriculum, college and career development, and year-round mentoring.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious UC Berkeley Law-affiliated program — 95% of participants go on to higher education. Combines legal education with paid internship.

internships.title: Student Volunteer and Intern Program
internships.description: Unpaid internship with the Judicial Council of California offering students experience in the policymaking body of California's court system. Available in fall, spring, and summer sessions across multiple divisions including legal services, court operations, and policy research.
internships.whyApply: Rare inside look at California's court system from the policymaking side — excellent for students interested in law or public policy.

internships.title: San Diego County Judicial Internship Program
internships.company: Superior Court of California, County of San Diego
internships.description: Judicial internship for high school and college students at the San Diego Superior Court. Interns observe court proceedings, assist with administrative tasks, and gain firsthand exposure to the judicial process. The Court Angels volunteer program provides additional service opportunities.
internships.whyApply: Direct courtroom exposure at one of California's largest court systems — see the justice system in action.

internships.title: Legal/Advocacy Internship Program
internships.description: Eight-to-ten-week unpaid legal internship at ACLU SoCal offices in Los Angeles, Santa Ana (Orange County), or San Bernardino (Inland Empire). Open to students at all academic levels including high school. Interns work on civil liberties cases, policy research, and community advocacy.
internships.whyApply: Work on real civil liberties cases at one of America's most influential legal organizations — available in LA, OC, and Inland Empire.

internships.title: Bloomberg Arts Internship Program
internships.company: LA County Department of Arts and Culture
internships.description: Eight-week paid summer internship connecting rising LA County public high school seniors with arts and cultural organizations. Interns work 3 days/week at an arts organization plus receive professional development, college prep support, mentorship, and cultural field trips.
internships.whyApply: Well-paid ($27.81/hr) arts internship backed by Bloomberg Philanthropies — combines cultural immersion with college preparation.

internships.title: Education & Engagement Paid Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship at Orange County's premier performing arts center. Interns gain exposure to arts programming, education operations, and youth engagement. Work alongside professionals in arts administration, event production, and community outreach.
internships.whyApply: Paid arts administration internship at a world-class Orange County performing arts venue — great for students interested in arts management.

internships.title: Silicon Valley Career Technical Education (SVCTE)
internships.description: Free hands-on career technical education program for 10th-12th graders across Silicon Valley. Offers 22+ UC-approved courses in electronics, manufacturing, automotive technology, construction, health occupations, and business. Students attend half-day sessions and earn industry certifications.
internships.duration: Full school year (morning or afternoon sessions)
internships.whyApply: Free UC-approved career tech program with 22+ hands-on courses and industry certifications — ideal for students exploring skilled trades.

internships.title: Student Leaders Program – California
internships.description: Six-week paid summer internship placing recent high school graduates at local nonprofit organizations. Includes a 3-day national Leadership Summit with Bank of America leaders. Students develop professional skills while addressing community needs in areas like financial literacy and economic mobility.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious Bank of America program combining paid nonprofit work with a national leadership summit — strong for finance-interested students.

internships.title: Student Leaders Program – San Francisco Bay Area
internships.description: Six-week paid summer internship at a Bay Area nonprofit organization with Bank of America's Student Leaders program. Participants attend a national Leadership Summit, build professional and leadership skills, and explore how public and private sectors address community needs.
internships.whyApply: Paid Bay Area placement connecting finance-minded students with nonprofit leadership experience and a national summit.

internships.title: California Youth Apprenticeship Program
internships.description: State-supported youth apprenticeship programs combining paid on-the-job training with classroom instruction for young people ages 16-24. Available in manufacturing, healthcare, IT, construction, and other industries across California. Programs lead to nationally recognized journey-level certifications.
internships.whyApply: State-backed paid apprenticeships leading to nationally recognized certifications — a direct pathway to skilled careers.

internships.title: LAUNCH Apprenticeship Network
internships.description: Multi-sector youth apprenticeship program registered in over 20 occupations from manufacturing to cybersecurity to healthcare. Partners with San Bernardino City Unified School District for high school apprenticeships. Combines paid work experience with industry credential preparation.
internships.whyApply: Earn while you learn across 20+ career paths including cybersecurity and advanced manufacturing — great Inland Empire opportunity.

internships.title: San Diego Symphony High School Ambassadors
internships.company: Jacobs Music Center / San Diego Symphony
internships.description: Two-week paid summer internship designed to build college and career readiness for San Diego County high schoolers (ages 16-18). Combines training with on-the-job experience at the San Diego Symphony, developing skills in arts administration, event production, and audience engagement.
internships.whyApply: Paid performing arts internship at San Diego's premier symphony — develop professional skills while exploring arts careers.

internships.title: County of Riverside Student Internship Program
internships.description: Internship program for high school and college students in Riverside County government departments. Interns gain real-world experience in public administration, human services, environmental health, public works, and other municipal operations while developing professional skills.
internships.whyApply: Inside look at county government operations in one of California's largest counties — spans multiple departments and career paths.

internships.title: UCSF SEP High School Intern Program (HIP)
internships.company: UC San Francisco — Science & Health Education Partnership
internships.description: 8-week paid biomedical research internship for SFUSD high school juniors. Students are matched with a scientist mentor at UCSF to conduct an original research project in areas like neuroscience, cancer biology, immunology, or bioengineering.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious paid research internship at a top medical research university — real lab experience with scientist mentors.

internships.title: Cedars-Sinai Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-long healthcare volunteer program at one of LA's top hospitals. Students choose clinical or clerical tracks, interacting with patients and healthcare staff. 100 hours of service required over 12+ months, with shifts available weekly.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at one of the nation's top-ranked hospitals — choose clinical or clerical track for hands-on healthcare experience.

internships.title: UCLA Health High School Volunteer Program
internships.description: Volunteer at UCLA's Ronald Reagan Medical Center as a Patient Transport volunteer or Wayfinder ambassador. Transport volunteers assist with patient discharges and transfers; Wayfinders greet visitors and provide wheelchair escort assistance. Ages 16+.
internships.whyApply: Gain clinical exposure at a top-5 US hospital — see all areas of the medical center through patient transport or ambassador roles.

internships.title: Crystal Cove Conservancy Fire Ecology High School Internship
internships.company: Crystal Cove Conservancy / Irvine Ranch Conservancy / UC Irvine
internships.description: School-year internship (Oct-May) studying wildfire impacts on coastal ecosystems. Students collect data at burn sites, analyze soil samples in the lab, and investigate how fire frequency influences ecological recovery. Includes Saturday field days and Wednesday virtual meetings. Culminates in a public research presentation.
internships.whyApply: Unique fire ecology research program in Orange County — real fieldwork studying wildfire impacts with university scientists.

internships.title: Arroyos & Foothills Conservancy High School Training Program
internships.description: Two-week intensive conservation training for students ages 15+. Learn trail restoration, native/invasive plant identification, research-focused field work, data analysis, and conservation communications. Full 8-hour days in Southern California open spaces. Mentored by college interns.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on conservation fieldwork in the San Gabriel foothills — intensive training in trail restoration and ecological research.

internships.title: Stanford Young Investigators Summer Internship
internships.description: Prestigious summer research internship at Stanford for Bay Area high schoolers. Students work one-on-one with scientists in tracks like Oceans, Geology, Energy, or Environmental Science & Engineering. Projects range from battery efficiency to AI-based fire detection to soil ecosystem health. Highly competitive — only 10-20 students selected annually.
internships.whyApply: Work directly with Stanford scientists on real sustainability research — one of the most competitive high school research programs in the country.

internships.title: Enterprise for Youth Paid Internship Program
internships.description: Multi-year paid internship program for under-resourced SF high schoolers. Students gain 75+ hours of on-site work experience plus 15+ professional development hours across fields like healthcare, technology, art/design, finance, law, government, and environmental stewardship. Earn college credit through City College of San Francisco.
internships.whyApply: Paid internships across 8+ career fields with college credit — builds real work experience and professional networks for SF students.

internships.title: KP Launch Summer Internship — Kaiser Permanente Northern California
internships.description: 7-week paid summer internship introducing high school students to non-medical healthcare careers at Kaiser Permanente. Explore roles in finance, communications, hospital operations, IT, and more. Designed for underrepresented and low-income students in Northern California KP service areas.
internships.whyApply: Paid internship at one of California's largest healthcare systems — explore non-clinical careers in finance, IT, and operations.

internships.title: Grassroots Ecology Environmental Internship
internships.description: 9-week summer naturalist internship focused on habitat restoration and environmental conservation in the San Francisco Bay Area. Interns participate in hands-on restoration work, native plant propagation, volunteer event coordination, and environmental education programming.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on conservation work in the Bay Area — restore habitats and learn native plant ecology in Silicon Valley's green spaces.

internships.title: California Attorney General Student Intern Program
internships.company: California Department of Justice — Office of the Attorney General
internships.description: Internship at the California Attorney General's office for students interested in law and public service. Work alongside deputy attorneys general on civil rights, consumer protection, environmental enforcement, and criminal justice. Multiple office locations across CA including Sacramento, LA, SF, San Diego, and Oakland.
internships.whyApply: Work at the highest legal office in California — gain exposure to civil rights, environmental law, and consumer protection.

internships.title: Evodyne Robotics Academy High School Internship
internships.description: Hands-on robotics internship where high school students work at Evodyne's Research Lab, contributing to enhancing current robots and designing innovative new ones for educational and research applications. Gain experience in mechanical design, programming, and AI integration.
internships.whyApply: Work in an actual robotics R&D lab designing and building robots — rare hands-on engineering experience for high schoolers.

internships.title: California State Bar Internship Program
internships.description: Internship at the organization that licenses all California attorneys and regulates the legal profession. Gain exposure to legal ethics, attorney discipline, public protection, and access-to-justice initiatives. Available in SF and LA offices.
internships.whyApply: Intern at the body that governs California's legal profession — unique exposure to legal ethics and attorney regulation.

internships.title: Santa Clara Valley Medical Center Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Year-round volunteer program at San Jose's largest public hospital. Teens assist in patient care areas, clerical support, and community outreach. Gain direct healthcare experience in a Level I trauma center serving Silicon Valley's diverse population.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at Silicon Valley's major public hospital — experience a Level I trauma center serving a diverse community.

internships.title: CISA Cyber & IT Student Trainee Program — California
internships.description: Federal paid internship for students age 16+ enrolled in high school or college. Work on cybersecurity, IT management, and infrastructure protection at CISA's California offices. Gain hands-on experience in federal cybersecurity operations and earn GS-1 Student Trainee pay.
internships.whyApply: Paid federal cybersecurity internship — work on real national infrastructure protection at one of the most important US cyber agencies.

internships.title: USDA Pathways Internship Program — California
internships.description: Year-round paid federal internship for high school and college students interested in agriculture, food safety, conservation, and rural development. Work at USDA facilities across California on projects spanning plant science, food inspection, animal health, and natural resource conservation.
internships.whyApply: Paid federal agriculture internship — explore careers in food safety, conservation, and plant science at USDA facilities.

internships.title: LA Times High School Insider Summer Internship
internships.description: Fully funded summer journalism internship at the LA Times for high school students in Los Angeles or Orange County. Interns function as working journalists — reporting on breaking news, writing features, conducting interviews, and publishing stories. Runs June through August.
internships.whyApply: Work as a real journalist at one of America's most prestigious newspapers — publish stories read by millions.

internships.title: Stanford SHTEM Summer Research Internship
internships.description: 8-week fully remote summer research internship blending Science, Humanities, Technology, Engineering, and Math. For high school juniors, seniors, and early community college students. Work on interdisciplinary research projects with Stanford faculty mentors.
internships.whyApply: Remote Stanford research experience blending STEM and humanities — accessible from anywhere in California with need-based stipends.

internships.title: Youth Mental Health Academy — California
internships.company: Child Mind Institute / California Department of Education
internships.description: Summer program providing high school students with hands-on experience in mental health awareness, peer support, and youth advocacy through project-based learning, mentorship, and community engagement. Students learn about mental health first aid, crisis intervention basics, and peer counseling techniques.
internships.whyApply: Become a youth mental health advocate — gain skills in peer support and crisis awareness through a nationally recognized program.

internships.title: High School Emerging Researcher Experience
internships.description: Eight-week full-time summer research internship for academically outstanding rising juniors and seniors (16+ by program start). Work alongside faculty in translational research labs at one of the nation's top hospitals. Concludes with a poster presentation at the research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Full-time lab immersion at a top-ranked hospital in the Texas Medical Center with faculty mentorship and a final research symposium.

internships.title: Texas Children's Hospital Summer Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program at one of the nation's largest children's hospitals. Teens assist in patient care areas, family waiting rooms, and support services. Applications open each January.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at a top-ranked children's hospital in the US. Excellent pediatric healthcare exposure for aspiring medical professionals.

internships.title: STARS Summer Research Program
internships.description: Eight-week summer biomedical research program for rising high school juniors (16+ by June 1). Students work side-by-side with faculty in UT Southwestern research labs on projects in immunology, genetics, molecular pathogenesis, and more. Concludes with a final research presentation.
internships.whyApply: Research at a top-tier medical center in Dallas. Stipend provided. Excellent pathway for students interested in biomedical sciences.

internships.title: Communities In Schools Legal Internship
internships.description: Paid eight-week summer internship placing academically outstanding high school students from at-risk schools into professional legal environments. Includes enrichment programs, mentoring from practicing attorneys, and exposure to corporate law, litigation, and public interest law.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid legal internship for high schoolers. Work in real law firms with attorney mentors.

internships.title: Student Legal Academy
internships.description: Paid summer program for high school students interested in the legal profession. Features classroom-style settings, lectures from practicing attorneys, guest speakers from the judiciary, and hands-on exposure to county government legal operations.
internships.whyApply: Paid legal internship in one of the largest county attorney offices in the US. Direct exposure to government law and public service.

internships.title: Emerging Leaders Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer internship placing high school students in City of Austin departments and local organizations. Focus on leadership skills, professional development, and civic engagement. Multiple tracks available including STEAM, aviation, and entrepreneurship.
internships.whyApply: Paid city government internship with multiple career tracks. Great for students interested in public service and civic leadership.

internships.title: Dallas Works Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing 100+ Dallas high school students in positions at corporations, nonprofits, and government agencies. Includes Career Ready Workshops and Internship Prep Bootcamp covering professional communication, workplace conduct, and resume design.
internships.whyApply: Paid internship with pre-program professional development training. Places students across diverse industries in Dallas.

internships.title: Business Innovation Academy
internships.description: One-week intensive program for rising high school juniors and seniors exploring entrepreneurship, innovation, and leadership. Covers finance, marketing, sales, and AI through expert-led sessions, case challenges, and company visits to Fort Worth businesses.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on business and finance education at a top Texas business school. Great for aspiring entrepreneurs and future business leaders.

internships.title: High School Investor Challenge
internships.company: TCU LKCM Center for Financial Studies
internships.description: One-week residential program for high-achieving high school students interested in stock market investing. Students live in residence halls, take finance classes at the TCU Neeley School of Business, and learn investment analysis, portfolio management, and financial markets.
internships.whyApply: Immersive residential investing program at a top business school. Two session dates available.

internships.title: Financial Planning Academy
internships.description: Summer program for rising sophomores, juniors, and seniors teaching personal financial planning, investing basics, career exploration in financial services, and money management skills. Funded by the Charles Schwab Foundation.
internships.whyApply: Free financial literacy program funded by Charles Schwab. Great introduction to financial planning careers.

internships.title: High School Research Academy (HSRA)
internships.description: Five-week immersive interdisciplinary non-residential summer research experience at UT Austin. Students work in active College of Natural Sciences labs in biochemistry, biology, environmental science, genetics, neuroscience, genome engineering, data analytics, and ecology.
internships.whyApply: Work in real UT Austin research labs across multiple scientific disciplines. Strong for college applications.

internships.title: PATHS-UP Young Scholars Digital Health Program
internships.description: Free 3-week summer program for high schoolers interested in healthcare engineering and technology. Engage with research in digital circuitry, engineering, computer science, physics, and their applications in healthcare at Rice University.
internships.whyApply: Free program at Rice University combining engineering and healthcare. Excellent exposure to digital health research.

internships.title: Moran EcoTeen Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: HMNS's largest high school volunteer program for rising 9th-11th graders. Volunteers fill roles in Summer Camps and Museum exhibit halls, gaining experience in science education, environmental awareness, and museum operations.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at Houston's premier natural science museum. Build science communication skills and environmental awareness.

internships.title: Student Conservation Association Houston Urban Green Internship
internships.description: Paid internship connecting young leaders with hands-on opportunities in urban forestry, conservation, and environmental education in the Houston area. Build skills in conservation leadership while working on real urban greening projects.
internships.whyApply: Paid conservation internship with a nationally respected organization. Hands-on urban environmental work in Houston.

internships.title: McNay Art Museum Teen Art Guide Program
internships.description: Teen Art Guide (TAG) positions for high school juniors and seniors interested in museums and art. Gain experience in event planning, community engagement, facilitating educational programs, and assisting with social media campaigns at a premier Texas art museum.
internships.whyApply: Work at San Antonio's premier modern art museum. Build skills in arts education, event planning, and community outreach.

internships.title: ASPIRE Youth Entrepreneur Summer Camp
internships.description: Entrepreneurship camp where high school students learn to brainstorm business ideas, create business plans, develop marketing campaigns, and generate budgets, financial statements, and balance sheets. Hands-on business creation experience.
internships.whyApply: Learn real entrepreneurship skills including business plan creation and financial literacy.

internships.title: Jewish Family Service High School Summer Internship
internships.company: Jewish Family Service of Greater Dallas
internships.description: 180-hour summer internship at a Dallas-area nonprofit. Students gain practical learning, essential training, and significant experiences. Understand nonprofit agency operations and missions while building job and college readiness skills.
internships.whyApply: Substantial 180-hour nonprofit internship providing deep understanding of community service organizations.

internships.title: Dallas County Health and Human Services Internship
internships.description: Internship for high school students interested in public health operations and careers. Work in the Dallas County Health and Human Services department learning about epidemiology, health education, environmental health, and community health initiatives.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to public health operations at a county level. Great for students interested in public health careers.

internships.title: Bauer Business Summer Institute
internships.company: University of Houston Bauer College of Business
internships.description: Summer institute for high school students exploring business and technology careers. Programs cover finance, entrepreneurship, marketing, supply chain, and business analytics at one of Houston's leading business schools.
internships.whyApply: Explore business careers at a leading Texas university. Great introduction to college-level business education.

internships.title: Caring Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer volunteer program for teens at Houston Methodist Hospital in the Texas Medical Center. Volunteers assist in patient care areas, waiting rooms, and support services. Gain direct clinical environment exposure and patient interaction experience.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at Houston Methodist in the Texas Medical Center. Build clinical experience and patient care skills.

internships.title: SMS Summer Research Program
internships.description: 8-week lab-based summer research program at Baylor College of Medicine in the Texas Medical Center. Students work alongside Baylor researchers on biomedical projects. Tied to the Saturday Morning Science affiliate pipeline.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on biomedical research inside the Texas Medical Center — the largest medical complex in the world.

internships.title: Young Engineers and Scientists (YES) Program
internships.description: Year-long paid research program for San Antonio-area high school seniors. Students work on real research projects at SwRI paired with a professional scientist or engineer mentor, using state-of-the-art equipment across aerospace, chemistry, and applied physics.
internships.whyApply: Paid, professionally mentored research at one of the largest non-profit applied research institutes in the country.

internships.title: Summer Junior Volunteer Program
internships.description: Seven-week summer hospital volunteer program for high school students ages 15+ at Memorial Hermann-TMC. Requires minimum 50 hours of service and a 3.0+ GPA. Covers ethics, confidentiality, infection control, and patient interaction.
internships.whyApply: Structured hospital exposure inside the Texas Medical Center with real patient-facing roles.

internships.title: Zoo Crew Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Hands-on teen volunteer program (ages 13-17) spanning guest engagement, animal care, and education programs. Includes in-person training days, virtual meetings, and online learning across spring, summer, and fall sessions.
internships.whyApply: Rare teen access to animal care and husbandry duties alongside Dallas Zoo staff.

internships.title: Summer Law Intern Program (SLIP)
internships.description: Joint program with Dallas ISD placing high school juniors at law firms, corporate legal departments, and non-profits across Dallas for 4-8 weeks in June-July. Students assist with admin tasks, observe legal professionals, and complete a Summer Learning curriculum on professional norms.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid high school law internship — placements with real Dallas law firms and legal departments.

internships.title: High School Fed Challenge
internships.description: Research and writing competition where teams of high school students act as future economists. Teams author economics research papers with a chance at publication in the Journal of Future Economists. Supported by Dallas Fed's K-12 economics and financial literacy resources.
internships.whyApply: Federal Reserve credential for high school students interested in economics and finance — a direct line into the Fed's educational ecosystem.

internships.title: JA Finance Park
internships.description: JA's capstone personal financial planning and career exploration program. High school students select a career, define financial goals, and develop and commit to a personal budget in a simulation environment. Part of a broader JA high school pathway including JA Company Program and JA Titan.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on financial literacy simulation that translates directly into real-world budgeting and career planning skills.

internships.title: George A. Jeffries NanoExplorers Program
internships.description: Non-residential summer research program for high school students at UT Dallas. Selected participants engage with faculty, staff, and graduate students across the schools of Natural Sciences, Engineering, and Behavioral & Brain Sciences on authentic nanotechnology and STEM research projects.
internships.whyApply: Rare nanotechnology research experience for high school students — cutting-edge lab access at UT Dallas.

internships.title: Welch Summer Scholar Program (WSSP)
internships.stipend: All housing, meals, and lab expenses covered
internships.description: Five-week residential chemistry research program for Texas high school juniors and seniors at six host universities (UT Austin, UT Arlington, UT Dallas, University of Houston, Texas A&M, Texas Tech). Highly selective, with students performing university-level research under graduate student and faculty mentors. Concludes with a research paper and poster session.
internships.whyApply: One of Texas's most prestigious high school research programs — fully funded chemistry research at top state universities.

internships.title: Youth Adventure Program (YAP)
internships.description: One-week residential career-exploration camp for rising 7th-12th grade gifted and talented students. Classes taught by Texas A&M faculty and industry professionals across architecture, veterinary medicine, engineering, kinesiology, and more.
internships.whyApply: Taste-test multiple careers in a single week on a major flagship university campus.

internships.title: Hire Houston Youth (HHY)
internships.description: City-wide paid summer jobs and internships program for Houston-area youth ages 16-24. Eight-week, 32-hour-per-week placements across City of Houston departments, private sector partners, and non-profits. Applications via NeoGov portal.
internships.whyApply: Paid work experience with the City of Houston and top local employers — a strong first job for Houston teens.

internships.title: Rice Lilie High School Summer Entrepreneurship Program
internships.description: Two-week residential entrepreneurship program for rising sophomores, juniors, and seniors on the Rice University campus. Students build their own venture through customer discovery, prototyping, and business modeling; visit the Ion and Greentown Labs; and meet Rice admissions reps.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on venture creation in Houston's innovation district with Rice mentorship and admissions access.

internships.title: Engineering Design Experience
internships.description: One-week residential engineering program for rising high school juniors and seniors across Civil/Environmental, CS, Electrical, OREM, and Mechanical Engineering. Students work in teams on real-world engineering challenges in the Deason Innovation Gym using 3D printers, laser cutters, and power tools.
internships.whyApply: Small-cohort (~20 students) engineering immersion with makerspace access and SMU faculty mentorship.

internships.title: Young Filmmakers Program
internships.description: Four-pillar film education program for ages 13-18: a free Young Filmmakers Competition, Summer Film Camps led by professional filmmakers, a Festival & Conference Scholarship Program (nearly 450 badges for local students), and a TEKS-aligned Digital Storytelling program in Central Texas schools.
internships.whyApply: Multiple entry points into film-making — from free competition to professionally-led camps — at one of the country's top screenwriting festivals.

internships.title: Museum Ambassadors Program
internships.company: Fort Worth Museum of Science and History
internships.description: Long-term teen volunteer program for ages 16+ supporting public engagement, educational programs (Dinos Rock!, Space Day, Chemistry Week), special events in the Omni Theater, and hands-on activities in the Innovation Studios. Minimum 40 hours with a 1-year commitment.
internships.whyApply: Sustained museum volunteer experience with leadership opportunities and STEM outreach exposure.

internships.title: Teen Leadership Program
internships.description: 16-week leadership program for high school students ages 16-18 at the largest food bank in America. Teens take leadership roles in volunteer-based projects, inspecting and sorting food, repacking family-sized bags, and building home delivery kits while learning about food insecurity and nonprofit operations.
internships.whyApply: Sustained leadership experience with America's largest food bank — substantial community impact for college apps.

internships.title: Space Center U — High School Missions
internships.description: Three-day immersive Space Center U programs for students ages 15-18, including Planetary Discovery, Journey to Space, Science in Space, and Living and Working in Space. Students step into astronaut and space engineer roles with hands-on engineering challenges and tours of NASA Johnson Space Center.
internships.whyApply: Direct behind-the-scenes access to NASA Johnson Space Center through Space Center Houston's education programs.

internships.title: Clark Scholars Program
internships.description: The Clark Scholars Program is an intensive 7-week summer research experience for 12 of the most outstanding high school juniors and seniors from around the country. Scholars work one-on-one with Texas Tech faculty in any discipline, from engineering and biology to humanities.
internships.whyApply: One of the most selective high school research programs in the country — only 12 scholars accepted nationally each summer.

internships.title: Texas 4-H Youth Development Program
internships.description: Texas 4-H engages K-12 youth in all 254 Texas counties in hands-on learning projects across agriculture, livestock, STEM, food science, veterinary science, robotics, and leadership. Members can compete at county/state levels and attend Texas 4-H Congress.
internships.whyApply: The largest youth development organization in Texas — builds agricultural and leadership skills with a direct pipeline into Texas A&M AgriLife.

internships.title: Texas Senate Page Program
internships.description: Selected high school students serve as pages for Texas State Senators during legislative sessions. Pages deliver messages on the Senate floor, attend committee hearings, and observe firsthand how Texas state government operates from the inside.
internships.whyApply: Immersive experience inside the Texas State Capitol — invaluable exposure for future public servants and attorneys.

internships.title: Code2College High School Tech Internship
internships.description: Austin-based nonprofit Code2College trains underrepresented high school students in full-stack software engineering, then places them into paid tech internships at partner companies including Indeed, Google, Meta, IBM, and dozens of Austin startups.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid tech internships for high schoolers — C2C has placed hundreds of Austin teens into real engineering roles at major tech companies.

internships.title: Youth Internship Program
internships.description: TPWD offers summer internships at state parks, wildlife management areas, and fish hatcheries across Texas. Interns assist biologists with habitat management, wildlife surveys, fisheries research, and park interpretation.
internships.whyApply: Paid field work with state wildlife biologists — one of the best pathways into conservation careers in Texas.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer / Wildlife Explorers Program
internships.description: Teens ages 13-17 learn about animal care, conservation, and zoo operations while assisting the Fort Worth Zoo staff with guest engagement, enrichment activities, and biofact handling. Fort Worth Zoo is ranked among the top five zoos in North America.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on animal and conservation experience at a nationally ranked zoo — excellent for aspiring biologists and veterinarians.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Dell Children's in Austin offers teen volunteer opportunities assisting with patient activities, family support, and hospital services at the only dedicated pediatric hospital in Central Texas. Volunteers gain direct exposure to pediatric medicine.
internships.whyApply: Volunteer at Austin's premier pediatric hospital — invaluable experience for future pediatricians and child life specialists.

internships.title: DoSeum Teen Volunteer Corps
internships.company: The DoSeum — San Antonio's Museum for Kids
internships.description: Teen volunteers at the DoSeum facilitate STEAM activities for children, help run summer camps, and assist with exhibit interpretation. The DoSeum is San Antonio's premier children's museum focused on hands-on science, art, and discovery.
internships.whyApply: Combine STEM passion with teaching younger kids — ideal for future educators, engineers, or STEM professionals.

internships.title: Teen Docent Corps
internships.description: Trained teen docents lead Witte Museum visitors through exhibits on Texas natural history, South Texas heritage, paleontology, and science. Teens develop public speaking, interpretation, and research skills while engaging with museum collections.
internships.whyApply: Develop museum education and public interpretation skills at San Antonio's flagship natural history museum.

internships.title: Austin Nature & Science Center Youth Programs
internships.company: City of Austin Parks and Recreation
internships.description: The Austin Nature & Science Center engages youth through teen volunteer opportunities, summer naturalist programs, and conservation camps. Volunteers assist with live animal care, visitor education, and habitat restoration in Zilker Park.
internships.whyApply: Learn hands-on wildlife and ecology skills in Austin's central nature center — great for budding naturalists and conservationists.

internships.title: Bush School Student Programs
internships.company: George H.W. Bush Presidential Library & Museum
internships.description: The Bush Presidential Library offers student programs including the Civic Engagement Academy and archival volunteer opportunities for high schoolers interested in American government, diplomatic history, and public service. Students engage with primary source documents and attend speaker events.
internships.whyApply: Unique access to presidential archives and civic education programs at one of the 14 presidential libraries in the US.

internships.title: Teen Docent Program
internships.company: Amon Carter Museum of American Art
internships.description: Amon Carter Museum trains high school teens as docents to lead tours and interpret American art for museum visitors. Teen docents also plan teen nights, engage with curators, and learn art history firsthand with the museum's renowned collection.
internships.whyApply: Shape your public speaking and curatorial skills at one of the nation's leading museums of American art — free admission and world-class collection.

internships.title: Bullock Museum Teen Programs
internships.description: The official state history museum of Texas offers teen volunteer and educational programs including teen docent training, summer history academies, and exhibit interpretation roles. Teens learn Texas history from the Spanish colonial era to present.
internships.whyApply: Work at the state's official history museum — ideal for students interested in history, historiography, or museum careers.

internships.title: HGOco Young Artist Programs
internships.description: HGOco, the community engagement arm of Houston Grand Opera, offers student programs including Opera to Go tours, teen chorus participation, and mentorship with HGO resident artists. Programs introduce students to opera performance and production.
internships.whyApply: Work alongside Houston Grand Opera's professional artists — extraordinary opportunity for young classical musicians and vocalists.

internships.title: Bernard Harris Summer Science Camp (ExxonMobil BHSSC)
internships.description: A two-week residential STEM camp for rising 6th-8th graders held at university campuses including UT Austin, UT Arlington, Texas A&M, and others. Students engage in hands-on STEM activities taught by university faculty, focused on encouraging underrepresented youth in STEM.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded STEM residential camp founded by astronaut Dr. Bernard Harris — one of the best middle school STEM opportunities in Texas.

internships.title: Diversity and Leadership in Business Camp
internships.company: UT Austin McCombs School of Business
internships.description: A free residential summer program at UT Austin's McCombs School of Business for rising high school juniors and seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Students attend business lectures, company visits, case competitions, and mentorship sessions with Texas executives.
internships.whyApply: Free residential business immersion at one of the top-ranked public business schools in the nation — a direct pipeline to McCombs.

internships.title: Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: SAMA engages high school teens as art educators and event assistants, providing training in art interpretation, gallery teaching, and museum operations. Teens work with SAMA's encyclopedic collection spanning 5,000 years of global art.
internships.whyApply: Explore curatorial and art education careers at San Antonio's premier fine arts museum with a renowned global collection.

internships.title: Simons Summer Research Program
internships.description: Highly competitive 7-week summer research program for rising high school seniors to conduct hands-on research in STEM fields with Stony Brook faculty mentors. Students author a written research abstract and present a poster at the Simons Poster Symposium.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious high school research programs in the country. Simons alumni are highly competitive in Regeneron STS and ISEF.

internships.title: Garcia MRSEC Summer Research Program
internships.description: 7-week summer research program in polymer science and engineering for high school students. Participants conduct original research projects, attend lectures, and present findings. Many projects lead to Regeneron STS and ISEF submissions.
internships.whyApply: One of the best-pipelined programs for national science competitions. Strong track record in polymer and materials research.

internships.title: Science Honors Program (SHP)
internships.description: Free Saturday science program for academically talented high school students from the NYC metro area. Offers advanced courses in mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology, computer science, and astronomy taught by Columbia faculty and graduate students.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and most respected Saturday science programs in the country. Free tuition at an Ivy League institution.

internships.title: Partners for the Future
internships.description: Year-long research mentorship for Long Island high school seniors. Students work 10 hours/week in CSHL labs alongside world-class researchers on cutting-edge biology and genomics projects, culminating in a research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Work at a Nobel Prize-winning research institution. Extraordinary opportunity for students passionate about genetics and molecular biology.

internships.title: RockEDU Summer Science Research Program
internships.description: Paid 7-week summer research experience for NYC public high school students. Students conduct biomedical research in Rockefeller labs alongside graduate students and postdocs, exploring topics from neuroscience to immunology.
internships.whyApply: Paid research at one of the world's leading biomedical research institutions. Designed to increase access for NYC public school students.

internships.title: Legal Outreach Summer Law Institute
internships.description: Intensive 5-week summer program for rising 9th graders introducing underserved NYC students to law school, moot court competitions, and legal writing. Students develop public speaking and analytical skills while exploring law careers.
internships.whyApply: Rare opportunity for younger high school students to get serious legal exposure. Strong track record of students matriculating to top colleges and law schools.

internships.title: New York State Senate Student Programs
internships.description: Student Programs include undergraduate internships and the high school Senate Session Assistant program, placing students with NY State Senators to observe legislative sessions, conduct research, and assist with constituent services.
internships.whyApply: Direct exposure to state legislative process. Builds practical civics experience valued by top colleges and law schools.

internships.title: High School Fed Challenge
internships.company: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
internships.description: Team-based competition where high school students role-play as the Federal Open Market Committee, analyzing economic conditions and making monetary policy recommendations. Regional and national finals hosted at the NY Fed.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious economics competition with direct exposure to Federal Reserve economists. Excellent for students interested in finance, economics, or public policy.

internships.title: Ladders for Leaders
internships.company: NYC Department of Youth and Community Development
internships.description: Competitive 6-week paid summer internship program for NYC high school and college students in fields including finance, law, healthcare, tech, media, and government. Students receive professional training and work at leading companies and nonprofits.
internships.whyApply: One of the most competitive paid internship programs for NYC students. Access to elite employers like JPMorgan, Google, and NYC government.

internships.title: Summer Youth Employment Program (SYEP)
internships.company: NYC Department of Youth and Community Development
internships.description: Nation's largest summer youth employment program. Provides paid summer jobs for NYC youth ages 14-24 across diverse industries including healthcare, tech, government, and nonprofits. Participants gain work experience and professional skills.
internships.whyApply: Most accessible paid work experience for NYC teens. Open to students starting at age 14, including middle schoolers.

internships.title: Metropolitan Museum of Art Teen Programs
internships.description: Free teen programs including Career Labs, Teen Advisory Groups, and summer workshops at the Met. Students explore museum careers, develop art-making skills, and engage with curators, conservators, and educators.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to one of the world's greatest art museums. Free programs designed for teens interested in art and museum careers.

internships.title: Whitney Museum Youth Insights
internships.description: Paid year-long program where NYC high school students work with Whitney curators and educators to research exhibitions, lead public programs, and create art-based projects exploring contemporary American art.
internships.whyApply: Paid immersion in contemporary art with a landmark NYC museum. Develops public speaking, curatorial, and creative skills.

internships.title: GOALS for Girls
internships.description: Free STEM initiative introducing high school girls to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics through a two-week summer intensive at the Intrepid Museum. Features hands-on projects, mentorship, and site visits to STEM employers.
internships.whyApply: Dedicated program for girls in STEM at one of NYC's most iconic museums. Exposure to aerospace and engineering careers.

internships.title: Wildlife Conservation Society Project TRUE
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer research program for NYC high school students studying urban wildlife ecology at the Bronx Zoo and across NYC parks. Students work alongside Fordham University graduate students to conduct original ecological research.
internships.whyApply: Combines wildlife conservation with hands-on urban ecology research in NYC's largest parks. Paid opportunity at a world-renowned conservation organization.

internships.title: Brooklyn Botanic Garden Garden Apprentice Program
internships.description: Multi-year paid apprenticeship for NYC high school students to learn urban agriculture, horticulture, and environmental education. Apprentices work in BBG's Children's Garden teaching younger kids and tending crops.
internships.whyApply: Rare multi-year paid apprenticeship combining urban agriculture, education, and environmental stewardship. Strong mentorship pipeline.

internships.title: New York Botanical Garden Explainer Program
internships.description: Paid teen docent program at the New York Botanical Garden where high schoolers become science communicators, leading tours and educational programs about plants, ecology, and botany for visitors of all ages.
internships.whyApply: Develops strong science communication skills in a botanical research setting. Paid position at one of the world's leading plant science institutions.

internships.title: Feinstein Institutes Medical Research Summer Program
internships.company: Feinstein Institutes for Medical Research (Northwell Health)
internships.description: Competitive 8-week summer program placing high school and college students in Feinstein Institutes laboratories to conduct original biomedical research. Includes weekly scientific seminars and a final research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Research at Northwell Health's flagship research institute, home to cutting-edge work in bioelectronic medicine and immunology.

internships.title: Mount Sinai Center for Excellence in Youth Education (CEYE)
internships.company: Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
internships.description: Year-round STEM and biomedical mentorship program for NYC public school students, including a 7-week paid summer research internship in Mount Sinai labs. Students work on projects spanning cancer biology, neuroscience, and public health.
internships.whyApply: Long-standing pipeline program designed to increase underrepresented students in STEM and medicine. Paid, mentored research at a top medical school.

internships.title: Radio Rookies
internships.description: Immersive paid audio journalism workshop for NYC teens. Participants learn to report, record, edit, and produce their own first-person radio documentaries that air on WNYC and NPR stations nationwide.
internships.whyApply: Produce stories that actually air on NPR. Exceptional experience for students interested in journalism or audio storytelling.

internships.title: The School of The New York Times Career Discovery Programs
internships.company: The School of The New York Times
internships.description: Pre-college summer academy where high school students explore journalism, media, business, and policy careers with New York Times journalists, editors, and industry experts through immersive courses and site visits.
internships.whyApply: Direct mentorship from New York Times journalists and media professionals. Strong signal for students pursuing journalism or media careers.

internships.title: Genesys Works Houston High School Internship
internships.description: Year-long paid internship program for high school seniors. Students complete 8 weeks of technical and professional skills training then work 20 hours/week at major corporations in IT, accounting, or operations roles.
internships.whyApply: Paid corporate work experience and college prep for underrepresented high school students.

internships.title: Genesys Works Dallas–Fort Worth
internships.description: DFW chapter of Genesys Works provides paid senior-year internships at major corporations in IT, data analytics, and accounting, paired with 8 weeks of summer training.
internships.whyApply: Real corporate experience and professional network for DFW high school seniors.

internships.title: NASA Johnson Space Center High School Internship Program
internships.description: Competitive paid internship for high school juniors and seniors to work alongside NASA engineers and scientists on spaceflight, research, and mission support projects at Johnson Space Center.
internships.whyApply: Direct hands-on NASA experience at the home of human spaceflight.

internships.title: Texas FFA Youth Leadership Programs
internships.description: Texas FFA supports 150,000+ student members with agricultural education, Supervised Agricultural Experience (SAE) projects, leadership conferences, career development events, and ag-industry internships across Texas.
internships.whyApply: Largest agricultural youth organization in Texas with hands-on SAE project funding and competitions.

internships.title: Travis County Teen Court
internships.company: Travis County Juvenile Public Defender's Office
internships.description: Teen volunteers serve as prosecutors, defense attorneys, bailiffs, clerks, and jurors in real teen court cases, sentencing first-time misdemeanor offenders aged 10-16 in a peer-led restorative justice program.
internships.whyApply: Real courtroom experience with trained attorney mentors — unmatched pre-law exposure.

internships.title: Mayor's Summer Youth Employment Program
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer internship placing San Antonio high school students (ages 16-21) in city departments, nonprofits, and local businesses for career exploration and workforce readiness training.
internships.whyApply: Paid first job experience with mentorship in public service.

internships.title: Texas A&M Enrichment Experience in Engineering (E²)
internships.description: Residential summer camp introducing rising high school juniors and seniors to engineering disciplines through hands-on projects, labs, and industry field trips at Texas A&M.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on engineering exploration at one of the largest US engineering schools.

internships.title: SMU Gifted Students Institute Summer Youth Program
internships.description: Residential and commuter enrichment camps for academically talented students in grades K-12, with courses spanning STEM, humanities, arts, and leadership, taught by SMU faculty and graduate students.
internships.whyApply: Rigorous academic enrichment from a top Texas research university.

internships.title: Galveston Bay Foundation Youth Volunteer Program
internships.description: Students assist with habitat restoration, oyster reef building, marsh grass planting, water quality monitoring, and environmental education events along the Galveston Bay estuary.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on coastal conservation on one of the most ecologically important estuaries in the Gulf.

internships.title: Armand Bayou Nature Center Teen Volunteer Program
internships.description: Teen volunteers lead guided tours, assist with wildlife care, maintain habitats at the 2,500-acre urban wilderness preserve, and support environmental education programs for visiting school groups.
internships.whyApply: Deep ecology and wildlife education at the nation's largest urban wilderness preserve.

internships.title: Houston Bar Association Summer Law Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships placing high school students from under-represented communities in Houston-area law firms, corporate legal departments, and judicial chambers to expose them to legal careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid legal career exposure with top Houston firms and judges.

internships.title: Dallas Fed High School Fed Challenge
internships.description: Academic competition where high school teams analyze current economic conditions, present monetary policy recommendations to Fed economists, and compete regionally and nationally. Includes coaching, resources, and site visits.
internships.whyApply: Direct engagement with Federal Reserve economists on real monetary policy.

internships.title: TXRX Labs Maker Apprenticeship
internships.description: Paid summer apprenticeship at Houston's largest makerspace, training students in CNC machining, welding, woodworking, 3D printing, and advanced manufacturing alongside professional makers and instructors.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on fabrication skills and a paycheck at a leading makerspace.

internships.title: Texas House of Representatives Page Program
internships.description: High school students serve as pages during the biennial Texas Legislative Session, delivering messages on the House floor, supporting state representatives, and observing state lawmaking in action.
internships.whyApply: Front-row seat to state lawmaking during the legislative session.

internships.title: Heard Natural Science Museum Youth Volunteer Program
internships.company: Heard Natural Science Museum & Wildlife Sanctuary
internships.description: Teen volunteers support wildlife care, trail maintenance, exhibit interpretation, and education programs at the 289-acre sanctuary north of Dallas. Junior docent and summer camp counselor tracks available.
internships.whyApply: Wildlife rehabilitation and natural history experience in a genuine sanctuary setting.

internships.title: Girlstart Summer Camp Teen Leader Program
internships.description: High school girls serve as teen leaders and near-peer mentors at Girlstart's STEM summer camps for elementary/middle school girls, gaining teaching experience while deepening their own STEM skills.
internships.whyApply: Leadership, teaching, and STEM role-modeling experience — strong for education and CS applicants.

internships.title: Texas Comptroller Summer Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships in the state's chief financial office for students interested in accounting, tax policy, public finance, and data analysis. Includes exposure to economic forecasting and state budgeting.
internships.whyApply: Paid public-finance experience at the Texas state treasury.

internships.title: Texas Biomedical Research Institute SURE Program
internships.description: Summer Undergraduate Research Experience (SURE) also accepts exceptional high school rising seniors for 10 weeks of hands-on biomedical research alongside Texas Biomed scientists in infectious disease and genetics labs.
internships.whyApply: Real biomedical research in infectious disease at a top independent research institute.

internships.title: CPS Energy Summer Youth Employment Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships for San Antonio high school students at the nation's largest municipally-owned energy utility. Placements across engineering, IT, sustainability, communications, and operations.
internships.whyApply: Paid engineering exposure at one of the largest public power utilities in the US.

internships.title: Houston Area Urban League Youth Leadership Program
internships.description: Youth leadership and civic engagement program for Houston high school students, including mentorship, community service projects, college access workshops, and annual National Urban League Youth Summit attendance.
internships.whyApply: Deep civil-rights history paired with real leadership development and national network.

internships.title: UT Austin Longhorn Center for Academic Equity Bridge Program
internships.company: UT Austin — Longhorn Center for Academic Equity
internships.description: Summer enrichment and college readiness program for first-generation and underrepresented Texas high schoolers preparing for university STEM, humanities, and leadership coursework at UT Austin.
internships.whyApply: Strong academic bridge and mentoring pipeline into a top public research university.

internships.title: NYU ARISE (Applied Research Innovations in Science and Engineering)
internships.description: Free, selective 7-week full-time summer research program for NYC high school students. Participants work in active NYU Tandon labs across bioengineering, molecular engineering, robotics, computer science, AI and machine learning alongside faculty and graduate-student mentors.
internships.whyApply: One of the most prestigious free high school engineering research programs in NYC — direct access to Tandon labs.

internships.title: NYU CS4CS (Computer Science for Cyber Security)
internships.description: Free 3-week summer program for NYC 9th-11th graders training in digital forensics, steganography, hacking, and cryptography. One of the only free cybersecurity programs for high schoolers nationally. No prior CS experience required.
internships.whyApply: Best free cybersecurity pipeline into college CS and cyber programs.

internships.title: Morgan Stanley JumpStart High School Program
internships.description: Paid 6-week program for high school juniors and seniors in NYC introducing students to the finance industry through shadowing, workshops, and networking. Students gain exposure to sales & trading, wealth management, and investment banking.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid high school exposure to a top-tier Wall Street firm — competitive and highly selective.

internships.title: Futures and Options Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internships placing NYC public high school students at financial firms, marketing agencies, nonprofits, and corporate partners. Includes work-readiness training, resume and interview prep, and field visits to companies like EY and JPMorgan Chase.
internships.whyApply: Established NYC pipeline placing students at top business partners — strong alumni network.

internships.title: Bloomberg Arts Internship
internships.description: Paid 6-week summer internship for NYC public high school juniors placing them at major cultural institutions (museums, performing arts organizations, libraries). Interns rotate through departments and complete a capstone project under professional mentorship.
internships.whyApply: Most prestigious paid high school arts internship in NYC — rotates through top cultural institutions.

internships.title: Brooklyn Museum Teen Apprentice Program
internships.description: Paid teen programs including the Museum Apprentice Program, Creative Practice, Teen Night Planning Committee, and InterseXtions. Teens explore art, education, activism, and museum careers while contributing to real programming and exhibitions.
internships.whyApply: Paid teen program at a major art museum — exceptional for aspiring arts professionals.

internships.title: Weill Cornell HPREP (Health Professions Recruitment & Exposure Program)
internships.description: Free 10-week Friday-afternoon program held January–April. NYC high school students hear from physicians and health professionals, explore health career pathways, and engage in interactive sessions on medicine, public health, and research.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to Weill Cornell faculty and pipeline programs — excellent for aspiring doctors.

internships.title: NewYork-Presbyterian Summer Volunteer Program
internships.description: Summer-only volunteer program for students 16+ requiring 120 service hours across select NYP campuses. Volunteer roles include patient services, child life programs, and clerical/administrative support in clinics and nursing units.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on hospital experience at one of NYC's top academic medical centers.

internships.title: Brooklyn Navy Yard Employment Center Internship
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing Brooklyn high school students at businesses within the Brooklyn Navy Yard including tech startups, manufacturing companies, and creative agencies. Interns gain exposure to modern urban industrial work.
internships.whyApply: Unique hands-on exposure to modern urban manufacturing and industrial design at the Brooklyn Navy Yard.

internships.title: CUNY Career Launch
internships.company: The City University of New York (CUNY)
internships.description: CUNY Career Launch hires thousands of student interns across dynamic industries at $20/hour for 6 weeks. Placements span tech, healthcare, government, business, and nonprofit sectors. Open to CUNY students and graduating NYC high school seniors.
internships.whyApply: Large-scale paid internship pipeline connecting NYC students to industry partners at $20/hr.

internships.title: AMNH Lang Science Program
internships.description: Selective 5-year (Grades 8–12) science enrichment program at AMNH. Students engage in hands-on investigations, field trips, lab work, and mentored research alongside AMNH scientists across biology, earth science, astrophysics, and anthropology.
internships.whyApply: Exceptional long-term immersion in real museum science — one of AMNH's most competitive programs.

internships.title: New York Hall of Science Explainer Program
internships.description: Paid positions for high school and college students serving as floor educators at NYSCI, engaging visitors with hands-on science demos, exhibits, and workshops. Explainers develop STEM communication skills and deepen content knowledge through weekly training.
internships.whyApply: One of the oldest and most respected paid STEM youth programs in NYC — strong professional development.

internships.title: Carnegie Hall Weill Music Institute Youth Programs
internships.description: Carnegie Hall's Weill Music Institute offers programs for teen musicians including NYO2 (for 14-17 year-old orchestral musicians), NYO Jazz, and Musical Connections. Students receive world-class mentorship from Carnegie Hall artists and perform at major venues.
internships.whyApply: Unparalleled mentorship and performance opportunities for serious young musicians at Carnegie Hall.

internships.title: NYC Urban Park Rangers Junior Ranger Program
internships.description: Volunteer programs run by NYC Urban Park Rangers connecting youth with nature through guided hikes, ecological restoration, wildlife monitoring, and conservation education across all five boroughs. Teens can progress to leadership roles in the Junior Ranger program.
internships.whyApply: Hands-on urban ecology and conservation experience with NYC's professional park ranger corps.

internships.title: Genesys Works New York
internships.description: Year-long paid professional internship program for NYC high school seniors from underserved communities. Students complete 8 weeks of training in IT, business operations, or accounting followed by paid corporate internships at Fortune 500 companies.
internships.whyApply: Rare paid senior-year corporate internship pipeline for NYC students from underserved communities.

internships.title: Cooper Hewitt Teen Design Scholars
internships.description: Paid summer intensive at Cooper Hewitt for NYC high school students interested in design. Scholars learn design thinking, prototyping, and visual communication while collaborating with museum curators, educators, and professional designers on real-world projects.
internships.whyApply: Only Smithsonian design museum program — rare paid design career exposure for high schoolers.

internships.title: The Bell Voices Reporter Internship
internships.description: Paid journalism training and reporting internship for NYC high school students. Students learn reporting, news writing, research, interviewing, and investigative techniques, then publish original stories about the NYC public school system.
internships.whyApply: Student-led newsroom publishing real investigative reporting — excellent portfolio-builder for aspiring journalists.

internships.title: Cornell 4-H Career Explorations
internships.description: 3-day residential career exploration conference at Cornell University for New York 4-H members in grades 8-12. Students choose tracks spanning agriculture, animal science, veterinary medicine, food science, engineering, and natural resources, working with Cornell faculty and graduate students.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to Cornell faculty and labs in agriculture and life sciences through New York's 4-H program.

internships.title: NYPL Teen Internship Program
internships.description: Paid summer internship placing NYC teens in NYPL branches and research centers. Interns support literacy programs, help run summer reading, assist with tech training, and contribute to teen programming while gaining library science and public service experience.
internships.whyApply: Behind-the-scenes experience at one of the world's most iconic library systems while getting paid.

internships.title: Bank of America Student Leaders — NYC
internships.description: 8-week paid internship placing NYC high school juniors and seniors with local nonprofits like Boys & Girls Clubs and Habitat for Humanity. Includes a fully-funded week-long Student Leaders Summit in Washington, DC to develop leadership skills and meet peers nationwide.
internships.whyApply: Prestigious national leadership program with paid nonprofit placement and DC summit experience.

internships.title: NYU Tandon ARISE
internships.description: Applied Research Innovations in Science and Engineering - a 7-week fully-funded program for NYC rising high school juniors and seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Students complete bioengineering, robotics, or materials science research with NYU faculty mentors and present at a final poster symposium.
internships.whyApply: Fully funded engineering research program at one of the country's top engineering schools, designed for students who would not otherwise access university labs.

internships.title: Cooper Union Summer STEM Program
internships.description: 6-week intensive engineering and computer science program for rising high school juniors and seniors. Students work in small teams on a culminating design project (robotics, genetic engineering, digital VLSI, architecture, or chemical engineering) with Cooper Union faculty and graduate student mentors.
internships.whyApply: Rigorous hands-on engineering immersion at a legendary engineering school with a real capstone team project to put on college applications.

internships.title: Roswell Park Summer Research Experience Program for High School Students
internships.description: Competitive 8-week paid summer research program placing rising seniors in cancer research labs at one of the nation's oldest NCI-designated cancer centers. Students work alongside principal investigators on projects in oncology, immunology, pharmacology, and cancer genetics, and present at a research symposium.
internships.whyApply: Paid cancer research experience at a world-class NCI cancer center - exceptional credential for pre-med and research-oriented students in Western NY.

internships.title: NYS Assembly Intern Program
internships.description: Legislative internship in the NY State Assembly in Albany. High school seniors and college students shadow lawmakers, draft legislative memos, research bills, and observe committee hearings and floor sessions during the spring session. Limited high school slots available through constituent assembly member offices.
internships.whyApply: Direct access to state lawmaking - a rare credential for high schoolers interested in government, policy, and law.

internships.title: Thurgood Marshall Summer Law Internship Program
internships.description: Competitive 6-week paid summer internship placing NYC public high school sophomores and juniors from underrepresented backgrounds at top law firms and legal departments. Interns work on research projects, attend legal seminars, shadow attorneys, and explore law school and legal careers.
internships.whyApply: Paid placements at elite Manhattan law firms - a rare and life-changing credential for aspiring lawyers.

internships.title: Girls Who Code Summer Immersion Program - NYC
internships.description: Free 2-week virtual summer immersion program for high school girls and non-binary students. Students learn computer science fundamentals, build a final project, and meet women engineers at partner companies including Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, and JPMorgan Chase based in NYC.
internships.whyApply: Free, well-known CS program with a strong community of women in tech and direct exposure to NYC tech employers.

internships.title: All Star Code Summer Intensive
internships.description: Free 6-week summer coding intensive for young men of color in grades 9-11. Students learn web development, JavaScript, and design thinking, visit top tech companies in NYC, and build a final capstone project presented to industry mentors at a showcase.
internships.whyApply: Transformative free tech program specifically designed for young men of color with a tight-knit community and industry exposure.

internships.title: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory Urban Barcode Research Program
internships.company: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory DNA Learning Center
internships.description: Free year-long DNA barcoding research program for NYC high school teams.
